## Factor Score Norming — Overview

<div style="font-size: 0.6em; line-height: 1.3;">
The raw factor score `F` is put through a **three-stage normalization pipeline**,
estimated in the HCAP normative subsample (`normexcld == 0`) and then applied to
the full HRS 2016 sample:

1. **Blom transformation** — maps `F` to a normal score (`f_blom`) within the
   normative sample; a cubic spline model (knots at 5th, 35th, 65th, 95th %iles
   of `F`) is used to apply the transformation portably to the full sample

2. **Demographic adjustment** — survey-weighted GLM regresses the Blom-transformed
   score on age splines (RCS centered at 70), sex, race/ethnicity, and years of
   schooling, plus two-way interactions (R² ≈ 0.37); fitted values = `Eblom`

3. **T-scaling** — standardized residual rescaled to mean = 50, SD = 10:

$$TF = 50 + 10 \times \frac{\text{Pblom} - \text{Eblom}}{\text{SEE}}$$

`TF` reflects **demographically-adjusted cognitive performance** relative to the
normative HCAP sample.
</div>

## Algorithm Flow Diagram

```{r}
#| fig-cap: "HRS 2016 classification algorithm (A7) with HCAP subsample counts"
#| label: fig-algorithm-v1
#| out-width: "100%"

knitr::include_graphics(here::here("Figures",
  "HRS-Core-Diagnostic-Algorithm-With-Ns-HRS-Sample-2026-04-22.png"))
```
